skill.name: iso-13485-certification
skill.description: Prepare ISO 13485:2016 Quality Management System documentation for medical device organizations, including gap analysis, quality manuals, procedures, and certification readiness materials.
skill.summary: Create and review ISO 13485 QMS documentation for medical device certification readiness.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static critical and high alerts as false positives from ISO clause numbers, Markdown formatting, and QMS template placeholders. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because it includes a local Python gap analyzer that reads user-selected documentation folders and can write a JSON report.

medium_findings.title: Local Documentation Scanner Reads User-Selected Directories
medium_findings.description: The included gap analyzer recursively scans files under a user-provided documentation directory and reads text or Markdown content. This is legitimate for ISO 13485 gap analysis, but users should avoid pointing it at broad folders that may contain unrelated confidential files. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for local filesystem access. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The script directly calls rglob and reads matching files, but there is no evidence of network transfer or credential harvesting.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly calls rglob and reads matching files, then optionally writes a JSON report. The behavior is expected for the skill and no exfiltration path was found.

medium_findings.title: Skill Workflow Instructs Local Python Script Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to run python scripts/gap_analyzer.py with a user-provided docs directory and output path. This is not command injection because the command structure is fixed, but it is a real execution path that should be disclosed for marketplace users. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for script execution workflow. Confidence: 0.74. Confidence reasoning: The execution instruction is explicit in SKILL.md, while the reviewed script does not invoke subprocesses or shell commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly documents a local Python command. Reviewed code shows local analysis behavior rather than shell expansion or arbitrary command execution.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives from ISO Clause Numbers and Templates
low_findings.description: Most critical and high static findings were false positives caused by ISO clause identifiers, QMS terminology, and template placeholders. Examples include clause identifiers such as 7.5.9.1 reported as hardcoded IP addresses, [LOCATION/SYSTEM] reported as Windows SAM access, and Markdown backticks reported as Ruby or shell execution. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for credential, network, weak-crypto, and prompt-injection intent. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: Targeted review found documentation and templates, not code paths for credential access, cryptography, reconnaissance, or network exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged locations are ISO clause references, document lists, or placeholders. No matching malicious behavior was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found in Reviewed Files
low_findings.description: Targeted review searched for override, pre-approval, skip-analysis, role-change, and elevated-authority phrases. No evidence found in SKILL.md, references, templates, or scripts. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE or not present for prompt injection. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: Keyword review plus contextual file inspection found normal ISO 13485 guidance and no instructions attempting to override the evaluator.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The searched files contain ordinary skill overview and workflow text. No suspicious system override language was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Documentation Scanner Reads User-Selected Directories
dangerous_patterns.description: The included gap analyzer recursively scans files under a user-provided documentation directory and reads text or Markdown content. This is legitimate for ISO 13485 gap analysis, but users should avoid pointing it at broad folders that may contain unrelated confidential files. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for local filesystem access. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The script directly calls rglob and reads matching files, but there is no evidence of network transfer or credential harvesting.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script directly calls rglob and reads matching files, then optionally writes a JSON report. The behavior is expected for the skill and no exfiltration path was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Skill Workflow Instructs Local Python Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the assistant to run python scripts/gap_analyzer.py with a user-provided docs directory and output path. This is not command injection because the command structure is fixed, but it is a real execution path that should be disclosed for marketplace users. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for script execution workflow. Confidence: 0.74. Confidence reasoning: The execution instruction is explicit in SKILL.md, while the reviewed script does not invoke subprocesses or shell commands.

content.value_statement: Medical device teams need structured QMS documents before an ISO 13485 certification audit. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create templates, review gaps, and prioritize certification work.
actual_capabilities: Explains ISO 13485:2016 clauses in practical language for documentation planning.
Guides creation of a Quality Manual with scope, exclusions, policy, and process references.
Provides CAPA and document control procedure templates for QMS document drafting.
Runs a local gap analysis script against user-provided documentation directories.
Identifies missing procedures, key documents, and prioritized remediation actions.
Supports certification readiness planning for medical device quality teams.
limitations: It does not replace an accredited certification body or qualified regulatory consultant.
The gap analyzer performs keyword matching and cannot judge document quality by itself.
PDF, DOC, DOCX, and ODT files are assessed mainly by filename unless extra extraction tools are added.
Users must validate generated documents against their products, jurisdictions, and actual processes.

use_cases.title: Build a Startup QMS Baseline
use_cases.description: Create a prioritized documentation roadmap, starting with the Quality Manual, quality policy, and core procedures.

use_cases.title: Assess Existing Procedures
use_cases.description: Run the local gap analyzer and review missing ISO 13485 procedures before an internal or external audit.

use_cases.title: Draft Audit-Ready SOPs
use_cases.description: Use templates and clause references to draft CAPA, document control, and other QMS procedures.

prompt_templates.title: Start My ISO 13485 Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create an ISO 13485 documentation plan for a medical device company. Ask for the device type, company stage, target markets, and existing documents before recommending priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Document Inventory
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a folder of current QMS documents. Guide me through a gap analysis against ISO 13485 and explain which missing documents are highest priority.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a CAPA Procedure
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CAPA procedure aligned with ISO 13485 clauses 8.5.2 and 8.5.3. Include purpose, scope, responsibilities, process steps, records, and placeholders I must customize.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Certification Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as an ISO 13485 documentation reviewer. Assess my QMS readiness, identify evidence needed for each major clause, and produce a prioritized audit preparation plan.

output_examples.input: We are a Class II device startup with no formal QMS documents.
output_examples.output: A phased roadmap that starts with the Quality Manual, quality policy, document control, record control, CAPA, complaint handling, risk management, and internal audit procedures.

output_examples.input: Check our current procedure folder for ISO 13485 gaps.
output_examples.output: A gap summary showing found procedures, missing procedures, missing key documents, compliance percentage, and recommended next actions.

output_examples.input: Help draft our document control SOP.
output_examples.output: A structured SOP draft with document approval, revision control, distribution, obsolete document handling, records, responsibilities, and customization notes.

best_practices: Provide your device type, target markets, company role, and existing QMS maturity before requesting documents.
Review every generated procedure with quality, regulatory, and process owners before approval.
Run the gap analyzer only on intended QMS folders and review the report for false positives.
anti_patterns: Do not copy template text into controlled procedures without matching actual company processes.
Do not treat keyword-based gap analysis as certification evidence by itself.
Do not include confidential device records unless they are needed for the requested review.

faq.question: Can this skill certify my company to ISO 13485?
faq.answer: No. It helps prepare documentation and readiness materials, but certification requires an accredited certification body.

faq.question: Does the gap analyzer upload my documents?
faq.answer: No evidence of network upload was found. The included script reads local files from the directory you provide.

faq.question: Can it create a complete Quality Manual?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides a template and guidance, but your team must customize scope, exclusions, responsibilities, and process references.

faq.question: Does it support FDA QMSR and EU MDR context?
faq.answer: It includes references to FDA QMSR and EU MDR as related regulatory context for medical device QMS planning.

faq.question: What file types can the analyzer inspect?
faq.answer: It reads text and Markdown content. For PDF, DOC, DOCX, and ODT, it mainly uses filenames unless expanded.

faq.question: Should I share confidential device files with it?
faq.answer: Share only what is necessary. The tool is local, but QMS documents can contain sensitive product and regulatory information.
